questions.q: What is the Preamble of the Constitution?
options: The introductory statement of the Constitution
questions.explanation: The Preamble is the introductory statement that sets out the ideals and objectives of the Constitution.

questions.q: With which words does the Preamble of the Indian Constitution begin?
questions.explanation: The Preamble begins with the words 'We, the People of India'.

questions.q: Which two words were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment of 1976?
questions.explanation: The words 'socialist' and 'secular' were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.q: The Preamble declares India to be a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic:
questions.explanation: The Preamble describes India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic.

questions.q: Which of the following is one of the ideals the Preamble seeks to secure for all citizens?
questions.explanation: The Preamble seeks to secure justice, liberty, equality and fraternity for all citizens.

questions.q: The Preamble is sometimes described as the what of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble is often described as the soul or spirit of the Constitution.

questions.q: Which value, relating to dignity and unity of the people, is mentioned in the Preamble?
questions.explanation: Fraternity, assuring the dignity of the individual and the unity of the nation, is mentioned in the Preamble.

questions.q: The word 'sovereign' in the Preamble means that India is:
options: Free from external control in its affairs
questions.explanation: Sovereign means India is fully independent and free from external control in conducting its own affairs.

questions.q: From which source does the Preamble declare that the Constitution derives its authority?
questions.explanation: By beginning with 'We, the People of India', the Preamble declares that the Constitution derives its authority from the people.

questions.q: The term 'democratic' in the Preamble indicates that authority in India rests with:
options: The elected representatives of the people
questions.explanation: Democratic indicates that the government derives its authority from the will of the people expressed through elections.

questions.q: The Preamble seeks to secure 'Justice' of which three kinds?
questions.explanation: The Preamble promises justice that is social, economic and political.

questions.q: The ideals expressed in the Preamble were drawn largely from which resolution moved in the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Preamble was based on the Objectives Resolution moved by Jawaharlal Nehru in 1946.

questions.q: The Preamble guarantees 'Liberty' of which kinds?
options: Of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship
questions.explanation: The Preamble assures liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship.

questions.q: The Preamble seeks to secure 'Equality' of which kinds for all citizens?
questions.explanation: The Preamble assures equality of status and of opportunity to all citizens.

questions.q: Besides 'socialist' and 'secular', which third word was inserted into the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment of 1976?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added the word 'integrity' to the phrase 'unity and integrity of the Nation'.

questions.q: Which date is mentioned in the Preamble as the date on which the people of India adopted the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble records that the people of India adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949.

questions.q: Fraternity in the Preamble is meant to assure two things; one is the dignity of the individual, and the other is the:
options: Unity and integrity of the Nation
questions.explanation: Fraternity assures the dignity of the individual and the unity and integrity of the Nation.

questions.q: In which year did the Supreme Court hold, in a landmark case, that the Preamble is a part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: In the 1973 Kesavananda Bharati case, the Supreme Court held that the Preamble is a part of the Constitution.

questions.q: The phrase 'secular' added to the Preamble indicates that the State:
options: Has no official religion and treats all religions equally
questions.explanation: Secular means the State has no official religion and treats all faiths equally.

questions.q: Which value listed in the Preamble appears last in the sequence justice, liberty, equality and ____?
questions.explanation: The Preamble lists the values in the order justice, liberty, equality and fraternity.

questions.q: How many times has the Preamble of the Indian Constitution been amended to date?
questions.explanation: The Preamble has been amended only once, by the 42nd Amendment of 1976.

questions.q: An early Supreme Court case in 1960 had held that the Preamble was not part of the Constitution; that view was later overruled. Which case held the earlier view?
questions.explanation: In the 1960 Berubari Union case the Court had held the Preamble was not part of the Constitution, a view later revised.

questions.q: The wording 'We, the People of India' in the Preamble reflects the source of authority underlying which constitutional concept?
questions.explanation: The phrase reflects the concept of popular sovereignty, that ultimate authority rests with the people.

questions.q: In the Kesavananda Bharati case, the Court indicated that the Preamble could be amended provided that which feature was not altered?
options: The basic structure of the Constitution
questions.explanation: The Court held the Preamble may be amended without affecting the basic structure of the Constitution.

questions.q: The ideals of liberty, equality and fraternity in the Preamble are often linked to the slogan of which historical revolution?
questions.explanation: The trio of liberty, equality and fraternity is associated with the ideals of the French Revolution.

questions.q: Which amendment number changed the description of India in the Preamble from a 'sovereign democratic republic' to a 'sovereign socialist secular democratic republic'?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 inserted 'socialist' and 'secular' into the Preamble's description of India.

questions.q: The Preamble's structure is generally divided into a description of the nature of the State, the objectives, and the:
questions.explanation: The Preamble describes the nature of the State, the objectives to be secured, and the date of adoption of the Constitution.

questions.q: The expression 'fraternity' was included in the Preamble partly on the suggestion of the chairman of which committee?
questions.explanation: The inclusion of 'fraternity' is associated with the Drafting Committee, chaired by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: The Preamble is generally regarded as which kind of guide to interpreting the Constitution?
options: A key to the minds of the framers
questions.explanation: Courts treat the Preamble as a key to understanding the intentions of the framers when interpreting the Constitution.

questions.q: Which constitutional value in the Preamble is most closely linked to Part III, the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: The ideal of liberty in the Preamble is given concrete effect through the freedoms guaranteed in Part III.
